title: Egg-in-a-Hole Toast

subs.name: thick multigrain bread or sourdough bread for more structure
ghee (desi ghee gives a rich, nutty flavour) or any oil for a dairy-free version

ingredients.name: green chilli sauce or hot sauce (optional)

steps.text: Using a round cookie cutter, the mouth of a glass or a knife, cut a circle from the centre of each bread slice. The circle should be about 6–7 cm wide. Keep the cut-out round — you will toast it alongside.
Melt half the butter in a frying pan or tawa on medium flame. Place two bread frames and their cut-out rounds into the pan. Let the bread fry for about 90 seconds until golden on the underside.

Flip both the frames and the rounds. Reduce the flame to medium-low. Crack one egg into each hole in the bread frame. Season immediately with salt and pepper. Cover the pan and cook for 2–3 minutes — the whites should be fully set while the yolk remains runny. For a hard yolk, give it an extra 1–2 minutes.
Slide two frames with their toasted rounds onto each plate. Serve immediately with hot sauce on the side. The cut-out rounds are perfect for dipping into a still-runny yolk.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: butter → ghee (desi ghee gives a rich, nutty flavour) or any oil for a dairy-free version; bread → thick multigrain bread or sourdough bread for more structure
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use any thick bread available — Britannia or local bakery bread works well. Replace butter with ghee (nei/ghee) for a richer flavour familiar to Indian palates; ghee also tolerates higher heat without browning too quickly. Use a kadai lid or plate to cover the pan while the egg sets. Add green chilli sauce or sprinkle red chilli flakes over the yolk for an Indian heat upgrade.
